summary: Matched several controller-related functions in sysdolphin/baselib, most notably HSD_PadFlushQueue, HSD_PadRenewCopyStatus, and the function previously named HSD_PadZeroQueue after identifying it as HSD_PadRenewGameStatus. The PR also corrected the associated BSS object from HSD_PadRumbleData to HSD_PadGameStatus, updated external users in fighter code and lbcardgame assembly, and adjusted controller.h to expose the new game status array and function name.
what_changed: Implemented HSD_PadFlushQueue in C, including merge, throwaway, and leave-one queue flush modes protected by OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts.
Implemented HSD_PadRenewCopyStatus, copying master pad state into HSD_PadCopyStatus, zeroing fields on error, and maintaining last_button, trigger, release, repeat, and repeat_count behavior.
Renamed the previously labeled HSD_PadZeroQueue function to HSD_PadRenewGameStatus because the old name did not fit the matched behavior.
Implemented HSD_PadRenewGameStatus with logic parallel to HSD_PadRenewCopyStatus, but writing to the newly identified HSD_PadGameStatus array.
Updated HSD_PadRenewStatus to call HSD_PadRenewGameStatus after renewing raw, master, and copy status.
Corrected the BSS symbol at 0x804C21CC from HSD_PadRumbleData size 0x114 to HSD_PadGameStatus size 0x110 in symbols.txt and assembly labels.
Updated fighter input reads in Fighter_Spaghetti_8006AD10 from HSD_PadRumbleData to HSD_PadGameStatus for sticks, c-stick, analog triggers, and held buttons.
Removed support_rumble from HSD_PadStatus in controller.h, consistent with the corrected 0x44-byte status layout implied by the 4-entry 0x110 array.
Added extern declaration for HSD_PadGameStatus[4] and renamed the public prototype from HSD_PadZeroQueue to HSD_PadRenewGameStatus.
Updated an lbcardgame assembly callsite to use HSD_PadRenewGameStatus instead of HSD_PadZeroQueue.
smart_moves: Used behavioral evidence from the decompiled function to reject the misleading HSD_PadZeroQueue name; the function renews a per-player game-visible pad status array rather than zeroing a queue.
Recognized that the BSS object previously named HSD_PadRumbleData was actually a fourth HSD_PadStatus[4]-sized array, HSD_PadGameStatus, and propagated that correction to fighter code and symbols.
Matched HSD_PadFlushQueue with a compact modulo-based queue pointer helper, replacing the earlier division-style expression while preserving ring-buffer behavior.
Kept interrupt masking around queue flush operations, which is important because the pad raw queue is shared with asynchronous input updates.
Matched copy-status and game-status renewal as near-parallel routines, which made the naming and data-flow relationship between HSD_PadMasterStatus, HSD_PadCopyStatus, and HSD_PadGameStatus clearer.
decomp_lessons: Do not trust early placeholder names when the function body contradicts them; HSD_PadZeroQueue sounded plausible but the matched code updated HSD_PadGameStatus and repeat/trigger fields.
BSS object size is a strong clue for controller structs: HSD_PadStatus[4] at 0x44 bytes each gives 0x110 bytes, which supported removing the extra support_rumble byte and renaming the object.
When a global controller status array is renamed, update every external consumer and the symbol map together to avoid preserving stale semantic names in unrelated systems.
For pad status renewal, preserve the exact order of field copies and zeroing; these routines are sensitive to struct layout and codegen.
Button repeat logic depends on comparing last_button and button, resetting repeat_count on state changes, and using repeat_interval when the countdown reaches zero.
Queue-flush matching can require modeling ring-buffer state transitions exactly: qread, qwrite, qcount, qnum, and the selected flush policy all affect generated control flow.
Header corrections can be as important as function matching because one stray struct field changes array/object sizes and invalidates BSS symbol interpretation.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_PadRenewGameStatus for the 0x80377B54 function, not HSD_PadZeroQueue.
Use HSD_PadGameStatus for the BSS array at 0x804C21CC, not HSD_PadRumbleData.
Controller renewal routines follow the HSD_PadRenew*Status naming pattern: HSD_PadRenewRawStatus, HSD_PadRenewMasterStatus, HSD_PadRenewCopyStatus, HSD_PadRenewGameStatus, HSD_PadRenewStatus.
Pad status globals use HSD_Pad*Status array names: HSD_PadMasterStatus, HSD_PadCopyStatus, HSD_PadGameStatus.
Flush enum names seen in the implementation are HSD_PAD_FLUSH_QUEUE_MERGE, HSD_PAD_FLUSH_QUEUE_THROWAWAY, and HSD_PAD_FLUSH_QUEUE_LEAVE1.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted HSD_PadRawMerge from four explicit button OR assignments into a for loop over four controller slots, matching the intended repeated pattern.
Used a modulo expression in HSD_PadRawQueueShift: *qptr = (*qptr + 1) % qnum.
Matched HSD_PadFlushQueue by keeping local qread and qdst PADStatus pointers and repeatedly merging queue entries while decrementing qcount.
Preserved the switch structure for HSD_PadFlushQueue over HSD_FlushType to separate merge, throwaway, and leave-one behavior.
Matched HSD_PadRenewCopyStatus and HSD_PadRenewGameStatus with explicit local pointers to master/copy or master/game status entries, which likely helped produce the target load/store pattern.
Assembly labels and .endfn names were updated in controller.s so the remaining asm reflected the corrected HSD_PadRenewGameStatus symbol.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for further controller.c matches around HSD_PadReset and HSD_PadInit.
Search for remaining stale references to HSD_PadRumbleData or HSD_PadZeroQueue after this rename.
Investigate whether HSD_PadCopyStatus and HSD_PadGameStatus have distinct gameplay semantics beyond being renewed from HSD_PadMasterStatus.
Verify whether any remaining HSD_PadStatus layout fields need documentation after removing support_rumble.
